stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.
The model is associated with key AI technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Rocket Lab is buying Iridium’s satellite network for $8 billion to take on SpaceX
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Rocket Lab is acquiring Iridium Communications for $8 billion in a major industry move.
The acquisition merges Rocket Lab's launch capabilities with Iridium's established satellite communications network.
The strategic goal is to significantly strengthen Rocket Lab's position as a competitor to SpaceX in the space sector.

stories.title: NASA's X-59 "frankenjet" tests supersonic flight without the sonic boom
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: NASA is testing the X-59 aircraft designed for quiet supersonic flight.
The primary goal of the tests is to achieve supersonic travel without generating a disruptive sonic boom.
Successful technology could potentially allow supersonic flights to operate nationally.

stories.title: Agent-Native Immune System: Architecture, Taxonomy, and Engineering
stories.author: Dehui Li
bullet_points: The shift to autonomous, agent-native AI systems introduces a fundamentally expanded and complex threat landscape.
Traditional AI defenses (like perimeter security and training-time alignment) are insufficient because they operate outside the agent's active reasoning loop.
New vulnerabilities include runtime hijacking through memory poisoning, tool-chain manipulation, and multi-agent attacks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: DeepSeek-V4-Pro is a newly trending model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting communication tools directly to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Comcast is splitting in two
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Comcast is planning a major corporate split, separating its media assets from its core infrastructure business.
The broadband and wireless division will remain under the 'Comcast' name.
The media and entertainment division, including NBCUniversal and Sky, will become a separate publicly traded entity.

stories.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is planning a massive military overhaul focusing on drone integration.
The entire half-million-strong military will be trained to utilize drones as a 'universal combat tool'.
This signals a significant shift towards autonomous and drone-centric warfare capabilities in the region.

stories.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The New York Times is reportedly challenging Microsoft's copyright claims related to its supercomputer built for OpenAI.
The shift in legal strategy follows a recent Supreme Court ruling that was unfavorable to Sony in copyright matters.
The story highlights ongoing, high-stakes legal battles concerning AI training data and copyright infringement.

stories.title: Govern the Repository, Not the Agent: Measuring Ecosystem-Level Risk in AI-Native Software
stories.author: Daniel Russo
bullet_points: Autonomous coding agents are increasingly operating at scale within shared software repositories.
Current evaluation methods focus on individual agent performance, ignoring systemic risks accumulated across the repository.
The research introduces 'integration friction' to measure ecosystem-level risk, questioning whether failures are agent-specific or repository-wide.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: The rapid acceleration of AI-assisted scientific discovery is outpacing the capacity of traditional human peer review.
The paper proposes using AI tools, such as Google's Paper Assistant, to automate and accelerate the scientific review process.
It introduces a taxonomy to frame the necessary transition toward AI-driven verification in scientific research.

stories.title: Vision-Default, Prior-Override: Causal Mechanisms of Perception-Knowledge Conflict in Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Michal Golovanevsky
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical challenge in Vision-Language Models (VLMs): resolving conflicts between visual input and pre-existing world knowledge.
It moves beyond behavioral observation by providing a component-level, causal account of how VLMs handle perception-knowledge conflicts.
The study employs advanced mechanistic analysis techniques, including activation patching across different model granularities (e.g., attention heads, MLP sublayers).

stories.title: Mechanism-Driven Monitors for Preemptive Detection of LLM Training Instability
stories.author: Yang Zheng
bullet_points: Addresses a critical, costly problem in frontier LLM training: undetected instability.
Proposes a novel approach using 'mechanism-driven monitors' derived from module functionality for early detection.
Aims to preemptively catch training faults before they cause significant computational waste.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports experimental local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: These camera-free smart glasses made me feel like Tony Stark
stories.author: Andrew Liszewski
bullet_points: Xgimi, a projector company, is entering the smart glasses market with the MemoMind One.
The glasses feature a privacy-focused design by omitting cameras, differentiating them from Meta/Snap offerings.
The product was showcased at CES 2026, indicating a recent market debut.

stories.title: China’s Z.ai claims it can match Mythos on cybersecurity
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2 model.
Researchers suggest GLM-5.2 matches Mythos in specific cybersecurity and bug-finding tasks.
The development signals a narrowing capability gap between Chinese AI models and Western leaders like OpenAI/Anthropic in specialized areas.

stories.title: Suno launches Spark incubator program to feed independent artists to its AI machine
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Suno is expanding its role beyond an AI music generator by aiming to become a streaming destination.
They launched 'Spark,' an incubator program designed to support and promote independent artists.
The Spark program offers grants, mentorship, and marketing assistance to unsigned musicians.

stories.title: Comcast is splitting its media and broadband properties
stories.author: Oliver Barnes and Daniel Thomas, Financial Times
bullet_points: Comcast is undertaking a major corporate restructuring by splitting its business units.
NBCUniversal and Sky are slated to be spun off into independent companies.
This move signals a significant shift in how Comcast manages its media and broadband assets.

stories.title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: DexCompose addresses the challenge of composing individual dexterous manipulation policies into multi-task skills using a single hand.
The core problem addressed is destructive interference caused by conflicting demands on overlapping fingers when stacking new tasks onto existing ones.
The proposed solution is a role-aware residual composition framework designed to effectively reuse pretrained dexterous policies.

stories.title: Agentic Hardware Design as Repository-Level Code Evolution
stories.author: Brucek Khailany
bullet_points: Introduces HORIZON, a self-evolving agent framework for hardware design.
Treats hardware design as a process analogous to repository-level code evolution.
Uses a specialized Markdown harness and git operations for state management and evolution.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: AI is accelerating scientific discovery, creating a bottleneck with traditional human peer review.
The paper proposes using AI to automate and accelerate the scientific review and verification process.
The work introduces a taxonomy to frame the transition toward AI-assisted scientific validation.

stories.title: Exposure Bias Can Alleviate Itself via Directional and Frequency Rectification in Flow Matching
stories.author: Shao-Lun Huang
bullet_points: The paper addresses 'exposure bias' in Flow Matching (FM) generative models, a known issue stemming from training/inference discrepancies.
The core contribution is DEFAR (DirEctional-Frequency Adaptive Rectification), a novel framework.
DEFAR proposes that exposure bias itself contains dynamic signals that can be used to guide its own self-rectification.

stories.title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: DexCompose addresses the challenge of composing individual dexterous manipulation policies into multi-task skills using a single hand.
The core problem identified is 'destructive interference' when new tasks conflict with existing manipulation outcomes on overlapping fingers.
The proposed solution is a 'role-aware residual composition framework' designed to effectively reuse pretrained dexterous policies.

stories.title: VGB for Masked Diffusion Model: Efficient Test-time Scaling for Reward Satisfaction and Sample Editing
stories.author: Molei Tao
bullet_points: Introduces MDM-VGB, a discrete diffusion sampler designed to improve Masked Diffusion Models (MDM).
The core innovation is augmenting unmasking generation with reward-guided remasking for efficient test-time scaling.
Draws inspiration from classical reward-tilted generation techniques, aiming to satisfy structural constraints or optimize downstream rewards.

stories.title: Democratic ICAI: Debating Our Way to Steering Principles from Preferences
stories.author: Shirish Karande
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in preference-based alignment methods, noting that pairwise labels obscure the underlying reasoning in human judgments.
It builds upon Inverse Constitutional AI (ICAI) to enhance decision-making interpretability by summarizing preferences into natural-language principles.
The research aims to improve upon existing ICAI methods by capturing the nuance of complex decisions that are missed by single-pass explanations.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: The paper addresses the scalability crisis in scientific peer review due to the rapid influx of AI-assisted research.
It proposes using AI itself to accelerate the verification and review processes within science.
The authors introduce a taxonomy to frame the discussion around this necessary transition in scientific validation.

stories.title: HPRO: Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization via Preference Extraction for Emotional Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Xiangmin Xu
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in current LLM-based Text-to-Speech (TTS) models: the tendency to produce statistically averaged prosody, lacking emotional expressiveness.
Proposes 'HPRO' (Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization) to improve emotional TTS generation using preference extraction.
Identifies and aims to solve structural mismatches in preference-driven optimization, specifically 'information conflict' between content and emotion gradients.

stories.title: Cognitive Episodes in LLM Reasoning Traces Enable Interpretable Human Item Difficulty Prediction
stories.author: Dawei Zhou
bullet_points: The research proposes a novel method to predict human item difficulty in educational assessments.
It shifts the focus from item text alone to observable cognitive processes induced by the problem-solving burden.
This approach aims to reduce reliance on expensive human calibration for fair test construction.

stories.title: From Tokens to States: LLMs as a Special Case of World Models and the Continuous Path Beyond
stories.author: Paul Dubois
bullet_points: The paper challenges the binary view separating LLMs (token predictors) and World Models (reality simulators).
It posits that LLMs can be viewed as a degenerate special case of World Models.
The work supports the idea that achieving general intelligence may require moving beyond purely autoregressive token prediction.

stories.title: MultiHashFormer: Hash-based Generative Language Models
stories.author: Nikolaos Aletras
bullet_points: Introduces MultiHashFormer, a novel framework for hash-based Generative Language Models (LMs).
Addresses the limitation of prior hashing methods (many-to-one collisions) that prevented their use in causal LMs.
Allows for hash-based autoregression, enabling parameter efficiency while maintaining generative capabilities.

stories.title: Can LLMs Judge Better Than They Generate? Evaluating Task Asymmetry, Mechanistic Interpretability and Transferability for In-Context QA
stories.author: Sambaran Bandyopadhyay
bullet_points: The paper challenges the common assumption that LLM evaluation (LLM-as-a-Judge) is inherently easier than generation.
It tests this asymmetry in a controlled in-context QA setting, isolating performance based solely on provided context.
Findings show that generation accuracy surpassed self-evaluation accuracy on three out of four tested benchmarks.

stories.title: Instagram is incorporating users' photos in ads for Meta Glasses
stories.author: notRobot
bullet_points: Meta is integrating user-provided photos into advertisements for its Meta Glasses.
This signals a move toward more personalized and immersive advertising experiences.
The development raises privacy and data usage concerns regarding user content.

stories.title: WATaBoy: JIT-Ing Game Boy Instructions to WASM Beats a Native Interpreter
stories.author: energeticbark
bullet_points: A project named WATaBoy successfully uses Just-In-Time (JIT) compilation to translate Game Boy instructions to WebAssembly (WASM).
The JIT-compiled approach demonstrated superior performance compared to a traditional native interpreter for running Game Boy code.
This represents an interesting niche achievement in retro-computing emulation and performance optimization within web technologies.

stories.title: The Drift Protocol Hack: A Six-Month Social Engineering Operation
stories.author: pavelespitia
bullet_points: A major security incident occurred on April 1, 2026, targeting Drift Protocol on Solana.
The attack was a sophisticated, six-month social engineering operation.
The financial impact of the hack was substantial, resulting in approximately $285 million being drained.
